free_text: natCu and natV cross-sections measured by
quasi-monoenergetic neutrons from p+7Li reaction in
the energy range of 18-34 MeV
Cyclotron was used for proton
acceleration.
For neutron beam production protons with
energies from 20 to 35 MeV were directed at a 2 mm
thick lithium foil with a 1 cm carbon backing.
The spectrum shape was calculated by MCNPX and
normalized by using the Be-7 residual activity and
the fraction of the neutrons to the forward direction.
Width of the monoenergetic peak
Samples (made from high purity (99.9%) metal) had
the disc shape with a 15 mm diameter.
Experimental cross sections were obtained by
adjusting EAF-2010 cross sections by a modified
version of SAND-II such that the adjusted cross
sections folded by the neutron spectrum reproduce the
measured reaction rates.
Relative accuracy of sample weighting.
Error was calculated by summation in square
of all uncertainties.
SS+On
VS. SF3=4N in 014.
Thickness of Cu target was 0.1-0.15 mm. Target was
irradiated at 86 mm distance from neutron source.
Irradiation time was 8 h.
